characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Ace: Played straight, though subverted in "Dude, Where's My Horse?", where he's shown as being no good at being a cowboy.At least, initially. Big Brother Instinct: Towards Dil. Even as a preteen, he still cares about his younger brother. Chick Magnet: Tommy has a steady relationship with his girlfriend, Rachel for most of the series. In "All Broke Up", he hits it off with another girl, who is even interested in being his girlfriend. And it's revealed in "TP + KF", that Kimi had a crush on him when they were younger and even though she states she no longer has feelings for him, it's implied she does. Continuity Nod: Carries around a screwdriver-shaped keychain, a throwback to his trademark toy from Rugrats. Didn't See That Coming: When Chuckie renounces their friendship in "TP + KF" over an argument, a lot of people at school immediately step up to inquire about becoming Tommy's new best friend. Tommy is surprised to find that he's way more popular at school than he had thought.

characters.name: Tommy Pickles
characters.description: The Ace: Played straight, though subverted in "Dude, Where's My Horse?", where he's shown as being no good at being a cowboy.At least, initially. Big Brother Instinct: Towards Dil. Even as a preteen, he still cares about his younger brother. Chick Magnet: Tommy has a steady relationship with his girlfriend, Rachel for most of the series. In "All Broke Up", he hits it off with another girl, who is even interested in being his girlfriend. And it's revealed in "TP + KF", that Kimi had a crush on him when they were younger and even though she states she no longer has feelings for him, it's implied she does. Continuity Nod: Carries around a screwdriver-shaped keychain, a throwback to his trademark toy from Rugrats. Didn't See That Coming: When Chuckie renounces their friendship in "TP + KF" over an argument, a lot of people at school immediately step up to inquire about becoming Tommy's new best friend. Tommy is surprised to find that he's way more popular at school than he had thought.

characters.name: Chuckie Finster
characters.description: Anime Hair: Especially during his "Yu-Gotta-Go!" dream sequences. Bad Liar: Angelica basically has to drill him in how to be an effective liar in "Brother, Can You Spare the Time?". Big Brother Instinct: Towards Kimi, who doesn't always appreciate it. Big Brother Mentor: Chuckie has an odd role when it comes to wisdom: most of the time he's nebbish and insecure, but when the moment comes for it he's also often the guy who picks up on his friends' sensitive/emotional problems and gives wise speeches to members of the gang, most often to Tommy and Kimi. Occasionally he's the "lesson giver" when Susie's not around.This leads to aRunning Gagwhere Chuckie gives some insightful advice, and then admits he got it out of a fortune cookie / hallmark card.Funnily enough, while Chuckie often fills this role for Tommy, Tommy often fills this role for Chuckie. It's a cornerstone of their friendship. In fact, a couple of the speeches he gives to Tommy are of the "teaching a lesson that Tommy didn't realize he was already inspiring in other people" sort. This leads to aRunning Gagwhere Chuckie gives some insightful advice, and then admits he got it out of a fortune cookie / hallmark card.

characters.name: Phil Deville
characters.description: The Big Guy: He's the most aggressive and hands-on of the group. Continuity Nod: His Season 1 shirt had a yellow duck on it like the clothes he wore as a baby. Full-Name Ultimatum: When he and Lil argue, they'll address each other by their full names. Gasshole: Demonstrated in one episode, but it's implied that he's like this. My Sister Is Off-Limits: When he finally learns the biological differences between him and his sister, he becomes overprotective of her and chases off a guy hitting on her.

characters.name: Lil Deville
characters.description: Beauty Is Never Tarnished: Discussed in "Truth or Consequences" when Phil blames her for cutting one while they're dressed as a two-headed alien during the shooting of one of Tommy's movies.Lil: It's biologically impossible for girls to fart. Deadpan Snarker: Especially when it concerns Phil's sloppy habits. Designated Victim: In "Dude, Where's My Horse?", she's constantly the butt of Tommy's bad cowboy skills. Full-Name Ultimatum: Same as Phil, but tends to invoke it more often. Hypocritical Heartwarming:Lampshaded by Tommy: "Lil disses Phil but she defends him when other kids do it".Discussed in "CoupDeVille": after some partygoers keep insulting Phil's hygiene, Lil initially says that only she can say those things because the other partygoers do not know him the way she does. However, she keeps defending Phil because she realizes that they aren't that different: not only does Lil admit to liking some grossout stuff, but, since Lil and Phil are twins,calling Phil gross would also be calling Lil gross.

characters.name: Angelica Pickles
characters.description: Adaptational Angst Downgrade: In the original series episode "Lucky 13", Angelica was emotionally devastated by the discovery that no one was coming to her 13th birthday celebration because they all chose to attend Savannah's party instead. In the Game Boy Advance gameAll Grown Up!: Express Yourself, the mission adapting the episode's events have Angelica infuriated and annoyed instead. Adaptational Nice Guy: Angelica is still a bit abrasive and crabby in the Game Boy Advance game, but the game depicts her as more willing to help the other children with their problems (to the point of holding the woman who conned Susie accountable in the mission adapting "Susie Sings the Blues"), and the mission adapting "Tweenage Tycoons" notably does not have her and Harold attempting to make money by selling shoddy ripoffs of Dil's inventions. All Love Is Unrequited: Swoons over a boy named Sean, but he doesn't even know she exists. A more notable example, maybe, is in "What's Love Got to do With It?" where she falls for her substitute teacher, of course, there's absolutely no hope for her, and the episode ends with her in tears. Subverted when, towards the end of the series, she develops an attraction to a recurring character named Darryl who seems to like her back. Anti-Hero: She's not as mean as she was in the original show and is portrayed more sympathetically, but can still be self-centered and rude at times. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Not to the same extent as when she was three, but she does still often pretend to be nicer than she actually is to get what she wants.

characters.name: Dil Pickles
characters.description: All of the Other Reindeer: "Brother, Can You Spare The Time?" shows him trying to interact with kids in his own grade, and they find him off-putting for his odd habits. Annoying Younger Sibling: While they generally get along, there are still quite a few times he gets on Tommy's nerves. Berserk Button: Make fun of his hat, and he will turn on you. It was Morty fooling around with his hat that caused Dil to stop tolerating his obnoxious behavior and to chew him out with"The Reason You Suck" Speech. Chaste Hero: Given his young age compared to the others, he's probably the only character on the show that doesn't really fall for anybody. Childhood Brain Damage: Dil's weirdness is hinted to be a factor of this. It's mentioned in "Truth or Consequences" that Phil and Lil dropped him on his head when they were babies.noteThough they could've beenalluding to the episodefrom the original series "Dil Saver", when they accidentally knocked over a computer which they thought Dil was trapped inside.In "Golden Boy", Grandpa Lou claims he was hit in the head by a fly ball at a baseball game. Didi, however, believes it to be genetic (from Stu's side of the family). Given what Stu and his family members are like, this might not be too unlikely.

characters.name: Kimi Finster
characters.description: The Ace: Sometimes when Tommy's not around. Disappeared Dad: Inverted. She knows where her birth dad is and keeps in touch with him, but is a continent apart from him due to her mom re-marrying Chaz.He even visits her in "Trading Places". Early Installment Character-Design Difference: She has a ponytail for the original "All Growed Up" special and the first season ofAll Grown Up!, while the second season onward instead has her hair in pigtails (albeit only two unlike her one-year-old self). Easily Forgiven: Despite the many times Kimi put her own overzealous interests over everyone else and gets way in over her head, people apparently let her off the hook, especially Chuckie. Meanwhile Kimi acts as if nothing major happened. Unfortunately, the same cannot be said for Tommy (or Angelica when she least deserves it). Far East: The chopsticks in her hair in Season 1; her focus on her Japanese heritage in Season 2 onwards.

characters.name: Susie Carmichael
characters.description: The Ace: She gets all of the good grades, she's a talented singer, and she's basically loved and respected by her peers.Deconstructed Tropein 'Susie Goes Bad Lite', as she reveals that keeping this image can be quite stressful at times. Badbutt: Her attempt to avert her "goody two-shoes" status in the aptly named "Suse Goes Bad Lite". It doesn't work out. Broken Bird: Almost became this whenshe got conned out of a thousand dollars. The Conscience: Occasionally plays this to the other kids in episodes where she has more of a supporting role, usually in contrast to Angelica beingThe Corrupter, with "Petition This" being among the more blatant examples - though in that one she learns that beingtoopushy with conscience giving can have consequences too. Cool Big Sis: Plays this role towards the others, though less as much than the original show.

characters.name: Harold Frumpkin
characters.description: Adaptational Jerkass:A mild case in the Game Boy Advance gameAll Grown Up!: Express Yourself, where the game's version of the events of the episode "Lucky 13" establish that Harold gave Savannah the idea to throw her party on the same day as Angelica's 13th birthday party and also frames Tommy of conspiring with Savannah to ruin Angelica's 13th birthday, when in the original episode he had nothing to do with Savannah's actions and was as appalled as the rest of the cast that this was happening to Angelica. That being said, he did this in retribution for how poorly Angelica had treated him earlier, he's remorseful of what he's done when Angelica finds out and the game ends with him making amends. All Love Is Unrequited: Possibly with Angelica. Ascended Extra: Started off as a minor character who only showed up in the original series' episodes "Preschool Daze", "Hello Dilly", and "Angelica's Assistant", is now a somewhat more prominent character who appears more often. Butt-Monkey: Often finds himself as this to Angelica. Extreme Doormat: An unfailinglyNice Guywho does pretty much whatever Angelica asks him to. Even her coldly rejecting him asking her out didn't stop him from leaving to get her a smoothie, much to Susie's surprise.Harold:I like doin' stuff for Angelica.

characters.name: Samantha Shane
characters.description: Dropped After the Pilot: Samantha only appears in the Rugrats special "All Growed Up." When the concept of that episode was made into a show, she was dropped in favor of Savannah. Expository Hairstyle Change: When she became Savannah, her hair is tied up in three pigtails on top of her head and is pulled back into a short ponytail.

characters.name: Savannah Shane
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Guy: Savannah in the original "Lucky 13" episode deliberately threw a party on the same date Angelica's 13th birthday party was being held just to be mean to Angelica. In the Game Boy Advance gameAll Grown Up!: Express Yourself, it wasn't actually her ideabut Harold'sand she willingly cancels her party after Angelica beats her in a volleyball match, in contrast to the original episode ending with her very angry at Angelica getting the last laugh. Alpha Bitch: She's the meanest character on the show and is one of the popular kids. Disproportionate Retribution: Why did Savannah decide to ruin Angelica's birthday party by throwing a party of her own on the same day? Because Angelica sat in her seat at the lunch table. Eviler than Thou: Savannah is this to Angelica, being far crueller and more manipulative than the latter. Her reasoning for hosting a party on the same day as Angelica's 13th birthday boils down toFor the Evulz. Girlish Pigtails: Her hair is fashioned in three pigtails similar to Kimi Finster's hairstyle, though her pigtails are a little shaggier than Kimi's.

characters.name: Rachel
characters.description: Satellite Character: By simply being Tommy's girlfriend and having a limited number of appearances, she never hangs out with Tommy when he is with his friends, nor does she have interaction with anyone else, aside from Didi in "Lost at Sea". Satellite Love Interest: Surprisingly averted here, several episodes actually flesh out Rachel a bit. Tragic Mistake: In "All Broke Up", Tommy's friends invited her over to try and make Tommy overcome his grief of her moving away, unaware that it was all an act to get his friends to stop bugging him about his nonexistent pain.When she meets another girl Tommy met named Anita, she decides to leave Tommy for good.

characters.name: Z
characters.description: Badbutt: Appears to be a rebel bad boy to the others, but he's really an okay guy who helps with charities. Dark Is Not Evil: He looks like the kind of punk you'd better run away from,but he's very fond of charity just like Tommy and co. Everyone Has Standards: After it comes to light thathe and Kimi were donating to a local charity and not stealing like Chuckie had originally assumed, Chuckie pleads for Kimi to forgive him. Kimiinstead snubs him. Z isshockedby this, and calls her out on it. Put on a Bus: Was phased out of the show shortly after his debut. Sheep in Sheep's Clothing: He seems like an alright guy whenever he's with Kimi, but Chuckie is very suspicious of Z and overhears him talking on the phone to someone in such a way that sounds like he's doing shady activities.Turns out, he's donating electronic devices to less fortunate kids.

characters.name: Bean
characters.description: Dead All Along:He turns out to have been the ghost of one of the original settlers from Camp Everwood. Red Eyes, Take Warning: His eyes glow red whenleading the group through the underground mine shaft. They then glow white after Dil discovers a photo of the original settlers, revealing his true nature. Comes back when he decides to give the group a "last goodbye", though. Really Seven Hundred Years Old:He's actually a ghost and has been dead since the 1800s. The Stoic: He shows very little emotion. Took a Level in Jerkass: Assuming it wasn't just a prank, Bean pulls one hell of aJump Scareon the gang whenhe hijacks their video, showing unsettling images from the camp and then shooting them a ghastlyDeath Glare/Red Eyes, Take Warningcombo.And this was after acting cordially with them andseemingly passing on.

characters.name: Nicole
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: She appears in a few episodes of the first two seasons, then disappears completely. The Ditz: By her own admission, she spaces out sometimes, but it still doesn't explain why she took a chair with somebody still sitting in itwithout even realising that.

characters.name: Nicholas
characters.description: The Ditz: It doesn't truly hit home until he ties his hair into a lampshade.

characters.name: Morty
characters.description: Hate Sink: Since he doesn't show any likeable traits and the main characters are clearly annoyed by him, it's more than likely that the audience is supposed to dislike him. Jerkass: He's extremely rude and obnoxious to the other kids. Pungeon Master: The 'Master' part being debatable. "The Reason You Suck" Speech: Dil gives him one when he decides he's finally had it with his jerkish behaviour. Took a Level in Kindness: After Dil chewed him out for his behaviour, his uncle Pangborn mentions that he's become a nicer person.

characters.name: Sean
characters.description: The Ditz: He's not the most attentive student. Jerkass: Boy, does it show in "The Old and the Restless". He constantly puts down Grandpa Lou during the tour and doesn't even lift a finger to help Tommy and Justin escape from a malfunctioning exhibit, because in his own words, he's "too cool for that." The Slacker: He's very lazy. Those Two Guys: He's always seen with his best friend, Justin.

characters.name: Justin
characters.description: Satellite Character: Never seen without Sean. What the Hell, Hero?: Tells Sean this when the latter refuses to help Tommy in "The Old and the Restless".

characters.name: Lil Q
characters.description: Ink-Suit Actor: He looks just like his voice actor. Maybe Magic, Maybe Mundane: It's left ambiguous whether he's Cupid or he's a normal kid and it's just a coincidence that people fall in love after getting hit by his hacky sack. Physical God: Heavily implied to be a Cupid-esque figure trying to earn his wings, who is constantly denied because he keeps making people fall in love with the wrong people. Shipper on Deck: Whoever he hits with his hacky sack falls in love with the first person they see. Stealth Pun: Lil Q is a matchmaker. His voice actor's name is Lil Romeo. Do the math.

characters.name: Fridge
characters.description: The Big Guy: He's quite hefty. Gentle Giant: Is big and intimidating (to Chuckie, at least) but is pretty mild mannered overall.

characters.name: Shark
characters.description: Gag Nose: He has a REALLY huge honker. Meaningful Name: His fin-like mohawk, the feelings of fear he inspires in other players, and his almost habitual 'devouring' of opponents by beating them and taking their cards. Stealth Pun: His name is Shark. He plays a card game. This makes him acard shark!

characters.name: Ryan Rodriguez
characters.description: Irony: IsThe Voiceless, and is later seen signing up for a singing contest. Shout-Out: To afanartist of the same name. The Voiceless: He never talks.

characters.name: Wally Ramone
characters.description: Graceful Ladies Like Purple: She usually wears purple clothes and is described as sweet. The Lad-ette: If her fondness of Phil's boyish behaviour and her best friendship with Lil are any indication. Passionate Sports Girl: She's the reason why Lil got hooked on soccer in the first place. Tomboy and Girly Girl: Wally temporarily becomes the girly girl to Lil's tomboy in "A Deville House Divided". While Wally is usually a sports loving tomboy like Lil, this episode has her become much more giggly, flirtatious, and romantic when she enters a relationship with Phil. Lil even complains about Wally becoming a girly girl in one scene. Tomboy with a Girly Streak: She'sThe Lad-ettewho plays soccer and enjoys Phil's boyish mannerisms where most other girls would find them disgusting, but she has been seen wearing dresses and shirts with flowers on them, and is quite the romantic when it comes to hanging out with the aforementioned Phil.

characters.name: Stu Pickles
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: Remember all of those wacky mishaps with his inventions that practically made his character a fan favorite inRugrats? Don't expect to see much of that here. Though he certainly fared better than Drew. Ditzy Genius: He makes lots of inventions, but doesn't have a lot of common sense. Gadgeteer Genius: When his inventions work, that is. Older Than They Look: Despite being in his forties now, Stu looks more or less the same as he did a decade ago.

characters.name: Didi Pickles
characters.description: Art Evolution: She had a white stripe in her hair in the first season, which was removed in the second season. Character Development: She became a child psychologist and no longer goes by what Dr. Lipshitz says. Mama Bear: When Pangborn went on about how Dil should learn to be normal, Didi got in the ex-wrestler's face and yelled at him. So Proud of You: Often tells Tommy and Dil how proud she is of them.

characters.name: Grandpa Lou Pickles
characters.description: Cool Old Guy: In spite of his age, he enjoys doing athletic activities and keeping up with what the younger people are into. Deadpan Snarker:Didi: So, do you have all the emergency numbers?Lou: Let's see... 9... 1... er, what was the last number again? Demoted to Extra: Compared to the original series, Lou isn't as prominent compared to the original series and rarely shows up, being only speaking three times within the first four seasons (and being completely absent in seasons 2 and 4). This did start to get downplayed in season 5 where he gets a major role in "Golden Boy" and "Bad Blood". Old Soldier: A kinder example. Would relate this stories to the kids about his time in the war. Opposed Mentors: With Grandpa Boris. Downplayed, in that it's really a conflict of grandparenting cultures and styles... like it's been fora decade.

characters.name: Grandpa Boris
characters.description: Alter Kocker: Justified, as he is a Jew from Eastern Europe. Art Evolution: He now has visible sclera. Cool Old Guy: Best shown in "Blind Man's Bluff", where he is shown having a good time at Slosh Mountain when his vision is restored and dances to Susie's performance in the amphitheater. Generation Xerox: "Rachel, Rachel" reveals that, as a young adult, Boris had a best friend named Oleg who was a lot like Chas and Chuckie. Goofy Print Underwear: He wears yellow boxer shorts with pink spots, as shown in "Blind Man's Bluff."

characters.name: Charlotte Pickles
characters.description: Asshole Victim: Kind of hard to feel sympathy for Charlotte for losing her job after everything she's done and how she treated Jonathan over the years. Character Development: Charlotte seems a lot more involved in her daughter's life than she was in Rugrats, and has basically taken over Drew's role as the authority figure in the household. Corrupt Corporate Executive: After being heavily implied to be one inRugrats, she makes this confession in "Lost at Sea."Charlotte: All those unethical business practices...those shady backdoor deals I did...I know where the bodies are buried! Good Parents: Charlotte is much more involved in her daughter’s life than she was in the original show. She also dishes out effective discipline as well. Just the Way You Are: Averted. Somebody should have really told her this, because her cosmetic surgery winds up making her look very unnerving.

characters.name: Drew Pickles
characters.description: Demoted to Extra:InRugrats, he was one of the most prominent adults on the show, but here he's barely seen and doesn't even talk save for a few episodes (in "Interview With a Campfire", "Fear of Falling", and "In the Family's Way"). It's particularly noticeable when you realize that Michael Bell also voices Chaz, who appeared often.Averted in "Golden Boy", where he plays a major role in the episode with Stu. InRugrats, he was one of the most prominent adults on the show, but here he's barely seen and doesn't even talk save for a few episodes (in "Interview With a Campfire", "Fear of Falling", and "In the Family's Way"). It's particularly noticeable when you realize that Michael Bell also voices Chaz, who appeared often. Averted in "Golden Boy", where he plays a major role in the episode with Stu. Good Parents: Drew is much less of a pushover with Angelica than he was in the series. Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow: His hairline has receded during theTime Skip.

characters.name: Chaz Finster
characters.description: Amusing Injuries: He often gets injured for quick gags. Ascended Extra: Is arguably more of a main character here (aside from season 4) than in Rugrats. Bumbling Dad: Played with. He's still just as much of a dorky klutz as he was in the original series, if not more so. In spite his awkwardness he's got his parenting skills down pat. Butt-Monkey: Like father, like son, he suffers a lot. *Crack!* "Oh, My Back!": In middle age, he's subject to back injuries, especially if they are caused byBetty.

characters.name: Kira Finster
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: This is played straight in "The Finster Who Stole Christmas", where she eventually loses her patience with the unhelpfulness of the airline service and resorts to forcing someone to let her take their cab when she and Kimi get stuck in Mexico. Blue Oni: To her own daughter, with which she is occasionally contrasted (most obviously in "The Finster Who Stole Christmas"). Happily Married: Still with Chaz after all these years. Older Than She Looks: Kira doesn't look a day older than when she and Chaz first met inRugrats in Paris. She's 40.

characters.name: Betty Deville
characters.description: Action Mom: She's missing a step from her younger days, but she's still a capable wrestler and more than willing to show her skills at the drop of a hat. Chubby Mama, Skinny Papa: The former to Howard's latter, having put on considerable weight during the time skip. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: Sometimes. Who else do you know would cheer loudly when they DIDN'T win a raffle? To be fair, she had spent that one episode fearing that things would go out of whack because of an eccentricity in Mercury's orbit. Formerly Fit: During the originalRugrats, Betty had a muscular built, but in the episode "Runaround Susie", she admits she's overweight when she was telling Chaz about renewing her and Howard's wedding vows, saying that she's having her wedding dress taken out a lot. Mama Bear: Is very protective of her children.

characters.name: Howard Deville
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Tends to fall into humiliating moments whenever he's around. Chubby Mama, Skinny Papa: In contrast to Betty, Howard looks considerably skinnier than he did in the original series. Demoted to Extra: While never the most prominent adult in the original series, Howard gets even less presence and faces it even worse after season 1 where he stops having speaking roles completely, despite his wife Betty being one of the most prominent adults on the show in comparison. Vomit Discretion Shot: He gets sick fairly easily. Younger Than He Looks: Howard hasn't aged well. He's supposed to be 43, but the graying hair, receding hairline, and facial wrinkles make him come across as being in his 50s, or even 60 at most.

characters.name: Hiro Watanabe
characters.description: Disappeared Dad: Averted. He writes to his daughter Kimi often, and even visits her in "Trading Places".

characters.name: Mr. Beaker
characters.description: The Comically Serious: Inverted...he tries to make humour sometimes, but it ends up falling flat because of his monotone. I Was Quite a Looker: States he used to be a buff handsome man before takingthe testin "Bad Aptitude". The picture looks obviously photoshopped. Nerd Glasses: He has glasses and is a science teacher. Shout-Out: ToBen Stein's character inFerris Bueller's Day Off. Spock Speak: He always uses technical terms and speaks monotonously.

characters.name: Miss Guppy
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappeared after the pilot, replaced by Mr. Beaker. Stern Teacher: She's kind of strict.

characters.name: Miss O'Keats
characters.description: Official Couple: With Vice Principal Pangborn when Angelica gets them to meet in "Tommy Foolery". Large Ham: Always speaks like she's a Shakespearean actress on stage.

characters.name: Principal Estes Pangborn
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He seems to collect the brunt of Dil's shenanigans, and he doesn't like it one bit. Friend to All Living Things: When scolding Tommy and Chuck in "Susie Sings the Blues", he stops for a moment to gently let a ladybug out of his office. The boys are taken aback by this, to which he questions if they've ever seen compassion for animals. Gentle Giant: He seems very scary in person, but once he recites his poetry, that's when all doubt about his sensitive side vanishes. Hair-Trigger Temper: He'll flip out at a moment's notice from relatively minor frustrations. Seemingly a carry-over from his pro-wrestler days, since the most that will happen is property damage and over-dramatic yelling. Intergenerational Friendship: With Dil... sort of. He's still weirded out by Dil's strange behavior, but Dil bears no hostility towards him.

characters.name: Emica
characters.description: Dropped After the Pilot: Barring some posters in later episodes, Emica only appears in the Rugrats special "All Growed Up." Teen Idol: She's a teenage singer.

characters.name: Beverly Jones
characters.description: Adaptational Karma: While she got away with her actions in the show, theGame Boy Advance game's interpretation of events has the mission adapting "Susie Sings the Blues" end with Angelica pursuing her and calling the cops on her, afterwards it is implied that she got arrested. Karma Houdini: She suffers no repercussions for conning Susie Carmichael out of $1,000. Monster of the Week: She's a conwoman who scams Susie and only appears in the episode "Susie Sings the Blues".

characters.name: Armadillo Dave
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: Madtooth the Magician. Blind Without 'Em: He's near-sighted. Butt-Monkey: His civilian identity is a postal worker who's frequently bullied. The Klutz: He can barely move without bumping into something. Nerd Glasses: He wears glasses.
